perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall expire upon the earlier of the date the Transaction is consummated or eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall expire upon the earlier of the date the Transaction is consummated or twenty-four months from the date hereof, unless otherwise extended in writing by the Participant.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text extends the expiration date of the agreement from eighteen months to twenty-four months but also adds a condition that it can be extended further in writing by the Participant, which introduces an in-text contradiction to Section 14. This grants Participant unilateral control over the agreement's duration, contradicting the initial eighteen or twenty-four month limit and impacting other sections like clause 5, which limits Participant's actions for eighteen months.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Participant hereby covenants and agrees that for a period of eighteen months following the date hereof, it shall not, without the prior written consent of Originator (which will not be unreasonably withheld) directly or indirectly: (a) enter into negotiations, provide advice, provide any form of financial assistance (including, without limitation, debt or equity investments) or otherwise encourage, facilitate or consummate any transaction involving Target (whether structured as an investment, a purchase or assets, a purchase of debt or equity interests (including, without limitation, options, warrants or other securities or instruments convertible into or exercisable or exchangeable for debt or equity interests or equity appreciation rights, phantom equity or similar instruments or rights containing equity-like features), a merger, consolidation, combination, recapitalization, refinancing, restructuring, reorganization or otherwise) without the prior express written consent of Originator; or (b) initiate discussions with respect to the prospective employment of, or hire for employment, any employees of Target or any subsidiary thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement's expiration date from a fixed 18 months (in the original text) to 24 months with a unilateral extension option for the Participant. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which limits the Participant's actions to 18 months. The change grants the Participant the ability to extend the agreement beyond the initial 18 months, while the Contradicted Text imposes restrictions on the Participant's actions only for the first 18 months. This creates a strong inconsistency regarding the duration of the Participant's obligations, impacting enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Participant acknowledges and affirms that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it and that Originator shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity. In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that Participant has breached this Agreement, then Participant shall be liable for and shall pay to Originator the reasonable legal fees and expenses and court costs incurred by Originator in connection with any such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: Participant acknowledges and affirms that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it and that Originator shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, and Originator waives all other remedies available at law or in equity. In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that Participant has breached this Agreement, then Participant shall be liable for and shall pay to Originator the reasonable legal fees and expenses and court costs incurred by Originator in connection with any such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction within Section 8 by stating that equitable remedies are the *exclusive* remedies for a breach, while the original states they are *in addition* to other remedies. This contradictory part of the sentence completely reverses the initial intent and limits Originator's recourse in case of a breach. This change creates uncertainty about the actual remedies available to Originator and could significantly impact their ability to seek full compensation for damages.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Participant acknowledges and affirms that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it and that Originator shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, and Originator waives all other remedies available at law or in equity. In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that Participant has breached this Agreement, then Participant shall be liable for and shall pay to Originator the reasonable legal fees and expenses and court costs incurred by Originator in connection with any such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 8 explicitly states that equitable remedies are the *exclusive* remedies for a breach, and that Originator waives all other remedies available at law or in equity. This directly contradicts the original intent, which stated that such remedies are *in addition* to other remedies. Therefore, the changed text creates a direct conflict and inconsistency within Section 8 itself, which is the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be amended or waived, in whole or in part, except with the written consent of Originator and Participant. It is understood and agreed that no failure or delay by Originator in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver hereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or future exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power or privilege.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may not be amended or waived, in whole or in part, except with the written consent of Originator. It is understood and agreed that no failure or delay by Originator in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver hereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or future exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power or privilege.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text now states that the agreement may not be amended or waived without the written consent of only the Originator, effectively removing the requirement for the Participant's consent for amendments, contradicting the initial part of the clause in Section 10. This creates an in-text contradiction as it gives Originator unilateral control over changing the agreement's terms, creating an imbalance of power.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may not be amended or waived, in whole or in part, except with the written consent of Originator. It is understood and agreed that no failure or delay by Originator in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver hereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or future exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power or privilege.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for 'Participant' consent to amend or waive the agreement. The contradicted text, which is in the same location in the document, is the original clause which required both Originator and Participant consent. The change therefore creates a direct contradiction, as one version requires consent from two parties while the other only requires consent from one party, leading to uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party. The Perturbation Type is Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, which specifies that contradictions occur when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.

perturbation.original_text: Participant agrees that Participant will (i) use the Evaluation Material solely for the purposes of the Participation, (ii) not, directly or indirectly, use, <PAGE> disseminate, disclose, discuss or reveal any Evaluation Material, and (iii) otherwise treat and maintain in full confidence all Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: Participant agrees that Participant will (i) use the Evaluation Material solely for the purposes of the Participation, (ii) not, directly or indirectly, use, <PAGE> disseminate, disclose, discuss or reveal any Evaluation Material, unless required by a valid subpoena, and (iii) otherwise treat and maintain in full confidence all Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "unless required by a valid subpoena," the modified text allows Participant to disclose Evaluation Material, contradicting the promise to treat and maintain all Evaluation Material in full confidence. This creates an in-text contradiction in Section 2(a), rendering the confidentiality promise uncertain and impacting the overall purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Participant agrees that Participant will (i) use the Evaluation Material solely for the purposes of the Participation, (ii) not, directly or indirectly, use, <PAGE> disseminate, disclose, discuss or reveal any Evaluation Material, unless required by a valid subpoena, and (iii) otherwise treat and maintain in full confidence all Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the participant will (iii) otherwise treat and maintain in full confidence all Evaluation Material. The changed text adds "unless required by a valid subpoena," which creates a direct exception to maintaining 'full confidence' and allows disclosure under certain conditions. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. This creates an in-text contradiction because the participant is now allowed to disclose information under a subpoena while also promising to keep the evaluation material in full confidence. The exception introduced fundamentally alters the obligation of complete confidentiality creating a strong contradiction according to the 'Inconsistencies' definition.
